MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2020 Continued To Oct-12-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Meli Brown Notice Of Motion Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses (Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents)
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Defendant Meli Brown's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses (Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents).

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Meli Brown's (defendant) motion to compel responses to special interrogatories and request for production of documents (RFPs) and for monetary sanctions (motion) came before this court on November 30, 2020. The court ordered the parties to meet and confer and reserved jurisdiction on the merits. The parties submitted a joint separate statement on their meet and confer efforts.

The court has considered the moving and opposition papers, and the parties' joint separate statement. The court now rules as follows: the motion is DENIED.

The motion is untimely. Plaintiff Angela Armstrong (plaintiff) timely responded to the special interrogatories and RFPs by serving objections in March 2020. Objections need not be verified. (Code Civ. Proc., Secs. 2030.250(a), 2031.250(a).) Defendant moved to compel in October 2020, significantly more than 45 days after plaintiff served the responses. (Code Civ. Proc., Secs. 2030.300(c), 2031.310(c).) Where a party does not obtain trial court relief from the statutory deadline, failure to move for further answers within the statutory time forecloses further relief[.] (Weinstein v. Blumberg (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 316, 322.) The motion is denied for the additional reason that it is not supported by a separate statement that provides all the information necessary to understand each discovery request and all the responses to it that are at issue. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(a)(2), (a)(3), (c)(1)?(3).)

The court declines to sanction defendant for filing an unsuccessful motion to compel. Plaintiff has not requested sanctions and the court finds that circumstances exist that would make the imposition of sanctions unjust. (Code Civ. Proc., Secs. 2030.300(d), 2031.310(h).)

Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must email it to adrienneleight@hotmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Adrienne Rogers; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Defendant Meli Brown Notice Of Motion Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Discovery Responses (Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Robert De Vries for Plaintiff.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Meli Brown'S Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-12-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Meli Brown' Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Monetary Sanctions.

Motion denied as being moot since verified responses were served before the hearing. Sanctions denied because Defendants didn't reach out to Plaintiff's counsel before filing the motion. Defendants reserve the right to move to compel any insufficient responses via a subsequent motion.

The moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at davididaho14@gmail.com.
